title: Vera Therapeutics Announces Inducement Grants Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
content:
BRISBANE, Calif., April  10, 2026  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Vera Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: VERA) today announced that, on April 8, 2026, the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors (Compensation Committee) of Vera Therapeutics granted inducement awards consisting of non-qualified stock options to purchase 258,200 shares of Class A common stock and restricted stock units (RSUs) underlying 131,925 shares of Class A common stock to eighty-nine (89) new employees under the Vera Therapeutics, Inc. 2024 Inducement Plan (Inducement Plan). The Compensation Committee approved the awards as an inducement material to the new employees’ employment in accordance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).

Each stock option granted on April 8, 2026 has an exercise price per share equal to $40.33, Vera Therapeutics’ closing trading price on April 8, 2026. Each stock option will vest over four years, with 25% of the underlying shares vesting on the first anniversary of the applicable vesting commencement date and the balance of the underlying shares vesting monthly thereafter over 36 months, subject to the new employee’s continued service relationship with Vera Therapeutics through the applicable vesting dates. Each of the RSU awards will vest over four years, with 25% of the underlying shares vesting on each anniversary of May 20, 2026, subject to the new employee’s continued service relationship with Vera Therapeutics through the applicable vesting dates. The awards are subject to the terms and conditions of the Inducement Plan and the terms and conditions of an applicable award agreement covering the grant.

AboutVera Therapeutics
Vera Therapeutics is a late clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing treatments for serious immunological diseases. Vera Therapeutics’ mission is to advance treatments that target the source of disease in order to change the standard of care for patients. Vera Therapeutics’ lead product candidate is atacicept, a fusion protein self-administered at home as a subcutaneous once weekly injection that blocks both B-cell Activating Factor (BAFF) and A PRoliferation-Inducing Ligand (APRIL), which stimulate B cells to produce autoantibodies contributing to certain autoimmune diseases, including immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN) and lupus nephritis. Beyond IgAN, Vera Therapeutics is evaluating additional diseases where the reduction of autoantibodies by atacicept may prove clinically meaningful. In addition, Vera Therapeutics holds an exclusive license agreement with Stanford University for a novel, next generation fusion protein targeting BAFF and APRIL, known as VT-109, with wide therapeutic potential across the spectrum of B-cell-mediated diseases. Vera Therapeutics is also evaluating development of MAU868, a monoclonal antibody designed to neutralize infection with BK virus, which can have devastating consequences in kidney transplant recipients. Vera Therapeutics retains all global developmental and commercial rights to atacicept, VT-109 and MAU868. For more information, please visit www.veratx.com.

For more information, please contact:

Investor Contact:
Joyce Allaire
LifeSci Advisors

title: Is Vera Therapeutics (VERA) Still Attractive After An 87% One Year Share Price Surge
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

If you are wondering whether Vera Therapeutics at around US$40.93 is still offering value or if the best days are already priced in, this breakdown is for you. The stock has returned 0.2% over the last 7 days, 1.7% over the last month, a 15.8% decline year to date, and 87.7% over the last year, with a very large 3 year return that is well above 7x. Recent coverage around Vera Therapeutics has focused on its position within the biotech space and how investors are assessing future prospects relative to current pricing. This context is important because it can help explain why the share price has moved in short bursts despite a weaker year to date number. Based on Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Vera Therapeutics scores 3 out of 6. The next sections will compare what different valuation methods suggest about that score and will also point to an even richer way to think about valuation at the end of the article.

Vera Therapeutics delivered 87.7% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Biotechs industry.

Approach 1: Vera Therapeutics Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows, then discounts them back to today using a required rate of return. The result is an estimate of what the business might be worth based on its expected ability to generate cash.

For Vera Therapeutics, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $241.734 million. Analysts and extrapolated estimates point to free cash flow turning positive in the projection period, reaching $704.789 million in 2030, with intermediate years including both negative and positive values as the business profile changes.

Using these cash flow projections and discounting them back to today, Simply Wall St’s DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $436.15 per share. Compared with a current share price around $40.93, this suggests the stock is 90.6% undervalued based on this model alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Vera Therapeutics is undervalued by 90.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 62 more high quality undervalued stocks.VERA Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Vera Therapeutics.

Approach 2: Vera Therapeutics Price vs Book

For companies where earnings are not yet a steady guide, price based on book value can be a useful anchor because it compares what you are paying in the market to the net assets on the balance sheet. Investors usually expect higher P/B ratios when they see stronger growth potential or lower perceived risk, and lower P/B ratios when expectations are more cautious or risk is higher.

Story Continues

Vera Therapeutics currently trades at a P/B ratio of 4.83x. This is above the Biotechs industry average P/B of 2.32x but below the peer group average of 6.66x. Simply Wall St also uses a proprietary "Fair Ratio" model, which estimates what a more tailored P/B might look like after considering factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, company size, industry and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio approach can be more useful than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry because it adjusts for the company’s own profile rather than assuming all biotechs should trade on similar multiples. In this case, the Fair Ratio for Vera Therapeutics is not available, so it is not possible to judge whether the current 4.83x P/B suggests the shares are overvalued, undervalued or about right using this method.

Result: ABOUT RIGHTNasdaqGM:VERA P/B Ratio as at Apr 2026

P/B ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 19 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Vera Therapeutics Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives are introduced here as a simple tool that lets you set a story for Vera Therapeutics, link that story to your own forecasts for revenue, earnings and margins, and then see the fair value that drops out of those assumptions on Simply Wall St's Community page. There, millions of investors compare fair value to the current price, watch Narratives update when new news or earnings arrive, and see how one investor might argue for a much higher fair value for Vera Therapeutics while another, using more cautious assumptions, arrives at a far lower number.

Do you think there's more to the story for Vera Therapeutics? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!NasdaqGM:VERA 1-Year Stock Price Chart
